prompt: Implement scaled dot-product attention as a function `attention(q, k, v, mask=None) -> torch.Tensor` WITHOUT calling torch.nn.functional.scaled_dot_product_attention or any pre-built attention kernel. Inputs q, k, v have shape (B, H, L, D). Mask, if provided, has shape (B, 1, L_q, L_k) and is additive (large negative values where masked). Implement the canonical formula: softmax(q @ k.T / sqrt(D) + mask) @ v. Output shape: (B, H, L, D).

criteria.description: All pytest tests in unit_tests pass against the candidate implementation

reference_solution:
import math
import torch
import torch.nn.functional as F

def attention(q: torch.Tensor, k: torch.Tensor, v: torch.Tensor,
              mask: torch.Tensor | None = None) -> torch.Tensor:
    d = q.shape[-1]
    scores = q @ k.transpose(-2, -1) / math.sqrt(d)
    if mask is not None:
        scores = scores + mask
    weights = F.softmax(scores, dim=-1)
    return weights @ v
unit_tests:
import torch
import torch.nn.functional as F
from solution import attention

def test_output_shape():
    q = torch.randn(2, 4, 6, 16)
    k = torch.randn(2, 4, 6, 16)
    v = torch.randn(2, 4, 6, 16)
    out = attention(q, k, v)
    assert out.shape == q.shape

def test_matches_torch_sdpa_no_mask():
    torch.manual_seed(0)
    q = torch.randn(2, 4, 8, 16)
    k = torch.randn(2, 4, 8, 16)
    v = torch.randn(2, 4, 8, 16)
    candidate = attention(q, k, v)
    reference = F.scaled_dot_product_attention(q, k, v)
    assert torch.allclose(candidate, reference, atol=1e-5)

def test_causal_mask_blocks_future():
    torch.manual_seed(0)
    B, H, L, D = 1, 1, 4, 8
    q = torch.randn(B, H, L, D)
    k = torch.randn(B, H, L, D)
    v = torch.randn(B, H, L, D)
    causal = torch.zeros(B, 1, L, L)
    causal.masked_fill_(torch.triu(torch.ones(L, L), diagonal=1).bool(), float('-inf'))
    candidate = attention(q, k, v, mask=causal)
    reference = F.scaled_dot_product_attention(q, k, v, is_causal=True)
    assert torch.allclose(candidate, reference, atol=1e-5)
